origin.name: Lebanon
origin.death_registration: Ministry of Interior Civil Status Directorate
origin.key_document: Arabic death certificate from Ministry of Interior (requires certified English translation)
origin.source: FCDO Lebanon travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Beirut guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation is not widely available in Lebanon. Full body repatriation is the standard approach.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Arabic death certificate (certified English translation required)
special_rules: Political instability and periodic conflict in Lebanon can affect timelines and access.
All Arabic-language documentation requires certified English translation for UK acceptance.
Police clearance is required for all unnatural or suspicious deaths.
Contact the FCDO emergency line immediately: +44 (0)20 7008 5000.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Lebanese documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Beirut can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Lebanon to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Lebanon. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-4 weeks. Arabic translation, Ministry clearance, and exit permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
